summary: Completed stability-focused QA/code review of DinoBrain v2 changes. Findings: Codex hook duplicate-lock path can skip preflight instead of fail-closing; Claude Code integration is MCP-only and does not enforce pre-response OS context; action-gate required-tool checks still cover only legacy tools; node lifecycle apply=true writes review records rather than executing merge/hold/delete/provenance repair; behavior eval exits ok when golden file is missing; Observatory JSON graph fallback rejects v2 wiki index when SQLite is unavailable; create_source_chunk stores raw chunk_text without redaction/size bounds; os_begin_task can leave started tasks if context-pack construction throws. Type check passed after adding portable Node directory to PATH; eval:behavior currently returns ok/skipped because behavior-golden.json is missing.
decisions: Review remained read-only; no code changes were made.
v2 stability risk is mostly in fail-closed enforcement, automatic lifecycle execution, strict eval gating, and active-session MCP/tool reload guarantees.
next_steps: Patch hook duplicate-lock path to inject fail-closed unless a concrete sibling preflight report/context can be read.
Add Claude pre-response enforcement strategy or explicitly downgrade Claude support to MCP-assisted only in docs/verification.
Expand action gate required tools to the full v2 contract and use actual exposed tool lists, not a hard-coded legacy subset.
Make behavior eval strict by default or fail CI when golden cases are missing.
Support wiki index v2 in Observatory JSON fallback and add a regression test.
Add redaction/size limits to create_source_chunk and transactional failure handling around os_begin_task.
search_queries: DinoBrain release and installer alignment memory
